{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_323227d601d440339ef45764","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# bead-forge (`bf`)\n2\t\n3\t![bead-forge hero](hero.png)\n4\t\n5\t**bead-forge** is a Rust CLI that replaces `br` (beads_rust) as the bead management tool for AI-supervised coding workflows. It is a drop-in replacement for `br` — every command, flag, and output format is identical — with one critical improvement: **concurrent claiming is correct**.\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## The Problem It Solves\n10\t\n11\t`br` has a race condition at its core. Claiming a bead requires two separate operations:\n12\t\n13\t```\n14\tbr ready --json          # read candidates (snapshot)\n15\t  ↓  (race window)\n16\tbr update <id> --status in_progress --assignee <worker>\n17\t  ↓  (race window)\n18\tbr show <id>             # verify the claim stuck\n19\t```\n20\t\n21\tWith 20 NEEDLE workers running simultaneously, multiple workers read the same candidate list, all select the same top bead, and all issue `br update`. SQLite last-writer-wins — 19 workers hold phantom claims on beads they don't actually own.\n22\t\n23\t`bf` eliminates this with a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` SQLite transaction:\n24\t\n25\t```\n26\tbf claim --assignee worker-7 --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --harness needle --json\n27\t```\n28\t\n29\tThe entire read-score-update sequence runs inside one write lock. No two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n30\t\n31\t---\n32\t\n33\t## Architecture\n34\t\n35\t```\n36\tSQLite (live store)                    issues.jsonl (git artifact)\n37\t    │                                        │\n38\t    │  all reads and writes                  │  exported by bf sync --flush\n39\t    │                                        │  imported by bf sync --import\n40\t    ▼                                        ▼\n41\t  beads.db                            .beads/issues.jsonl\n42\t  (gitignored)                        (committed to git)\n43\t```\n44\t\n45\t**SQLite is the live database.** JSONL is a derived snapshot committed to git for backup, cross-machine sharing, and recovery. `beads.db` is rebuilt at any time with `bf doctor --repair`.\n46\t\n47\t**No daemon. No server. No flock.** Each `bf` invocation is self-contained: open DB, acquire write lock, execute, release, exit. SQLite WAL mode enables concurrent reads during writes.\n48\t\n49\t---\n50\t\n51\t## Key Features\n52\t\n53\t### Atomic Concurrent Claiming\n54\t\n55\t```\n56\tWorker 1: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT + score → UPDATE winner → COMMIT\n57\tWorker 2: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked until Worker 1 commits) → SELECT → ...\n58\t...\n59\tWorker 20: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT → NONE (queue empty) → COMMIT\n60\t```\n61\t\n62\tThe entire claim pipeline — stale reclamation, candidate scoring, winner selection, status update, event recording — runs in a single SQLite write transaction. Guaranteed correct under any concurrency level.\n63\t\n64\t### Critical Path Scoring\n65\t\n66\t`bf` computes the **float** of each bead: how many hops it can slip before delaying its epic. Beads on the critical path (`float == 0`) receive a 1000-point bonus in the claim scorer, ensuring the fleet works on the most impactful beads automatically — without human priority curation.\n67\t\n68\t```\n69\t$ bf critical-path bf-epic-123\n70\t\n71\t  float=0  [bf-a3f8] Implement auth token refresh        in_progress\n72\t  float=0  [bf-b2c1] Fix concurrent session state bug    open\n73\t  float=2  [bf-d5e3] Update API documentation            open\n74\t```\n75\t\n76\t### Velocity-Aware Scoring\n77\t\n78\tWorkers declare their composition when claiming:\n79\t\n80\t```bash\n81\tbf claim --assignee worker-7 \\\n82\t         --model claude-sonnet-4-6 \\\n83\t         --harness needle \\\n84\t         --harness-version 0.5.2 \\\n85\t         --json\n86\t```\n87\t\n88\t`bf` tracks close times per `(model, harness, issue_type)` in `velocity_stats`. The claim scorer weights candidates by `impact / expected_duration` — the fleet maximizes throughput per unit time, not just raw priority. After 10+ completions per cohort, routing becomes measurably smarter.\n89\t\n90\t```\n91\t$ bf velocity\n92\t\n93\tModel                  Harness   Type     Samples  p50    p90\n94\tclaude-opus-4-7        needle    task     142      8m     22m\n95\tclaude-sonnet-4-6      needle    task     87       18m    45m\n96\tclaude-haiku-4-5       needle    task     23       35m    2h10m\n97\t```\n98\t\n99\t### Atomic Batch Operations\n100\t\n101\tReplaces NEEDLE's crash-unsafe create+dep chains:\n102\t\n103\t```bash\n104\t# NEEDLE mitosis: split 1 bead into N children atomically\n105\t# Method 1: Dedicated mitosis command (recommended)\n106\tbf mitosis bf-a3f8 \\\n107\t  --children '[\n108\t    {\"title\": \"Implement login handler\", \"type\": \"task\", \"priority\": 2},\n109\t    {\"title\": \"Add session tests\", \"type\": \"task\", \"priority\": 2}\n110\t  ]' \\\n111\t  --reason \"Split into children\"\n112\t\n113\t# Method 2: Direct batch with placeholder references\n114\t# Use @0, @1, ... to reference beads created earlier in the batch\n115\tbf batch --json '[\n116\t  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Implement login handler\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n117\t  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Add session tests\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n118\t  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"@0\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n119\t  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"@1\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n120\t  {\"op\": \"close\", \"id\": \"bf-a3f8\", \"reason\": \"Split into children\"}\n121\t]'\n122\t```\n123\t\n124\t**Placeholder references**: `@0`, `@1`, etc. resolve to the IDs of beads created earlier in the batch. You don't need to know the child IDs in advance — `bf` substitutes them automatically.\n125\t\n126\tAll operations execute in one `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. A crash mid-batch leaves zero partial state — SQLite rolls back automatically.\n127\t\n128\t### Extensible Annotations\n129\t\n130\tArbitrary key-value metadata on any bead, transparent to `br`:\n131\t\n132\t```bash\n133\tbf annotate bf-a3f8 needle_attempt=3 needle_session=abc123 review_status=needs_review\n134\tbf list --annotation needle_attempt=3\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t### Operation History\n138\t\n139\t```bash\n140\tbf log bf-a3f8\n141\t\n142\t2026-04-29 10:00  CREATED     by: human                  \"Implement auth flow\"\n143\t2026-04-29 14:30  CLAIMED     by: worker-3 (sonnet-4-6)  open → in_progress\n144\t2026-04-29 15:45  COMMENT     by: worker-3               \"Found edge case\"\n145\t2026-04-29 16:00  CLOSED      by: worker-3               \"Completed\"\n146\t```\n147\t\n148\t---\n149\t\n150\t## Commands\n151\t\n152\t```\n153\tbf create        --title \"...\" --type <type> --priority <N> [--label ...]\n154\tbf list          [--status <s>] [--type <t>] [--assignee <a>] [--format json|text|toon]\n155\tbf show          <id>\n156\tbf update        <id> [--status <s>] [--assignee <a>] ...]\n157\tbf close         <id> [--reason \"...\"]\n158\tbf claim         --assignee <id> [--model <m>] [--harness <h>] [--harness-version <v>]\n159\t                 [--workspace <path>] [--any]\n160\tbf critical-path <epic-id>\n161\tbf batch         [--file ops.json] [--json '[...]']\n162\tbf annotate      <id> key=value [--remove key]\n163\tbf log           [<id>] [--since <date>] [--actor <a>] [--diff]\n164\tbf rotate        [--age-days N]\n165\tbf velocity      [--model <m>] [--harness <h>]\n166\tbf dep           add-blocker <blocker> <blockee>\n167\tbf dep           tree <id>\n168\tbf label         <id> add <label> [<label> ...]\n169\tbf sync          [--flush-only] [--import-only]\n170\tbf doctor        [--repair] [--check] [--reclaim-stale [--ttl <duration>]]\n171\tbf init          [--prefix <p>]\n172\tbf stats\n173\tbf search        <query>\n174\t```\n175\t\n176\tAll `br` commands work identically. `bf` is a strict superset.\n177\t\n178\t---\n179\t\n180\t## NEEDLE Integration\n181\t\n182\tReplace the five non-atomic `br` chains in `bead_store/mod.rs`:\n183\t\n184\t| Old (racy) | New (atomic) |\n185\t|-----------|--------------|\n186\t| `br ready` → `br update` → `br show` (3 calls, 2 race windows) | `bf claim` (1 call, 1 transaction) |\n187\t| `br create` + `br dep add` (orphan if crash between) | `bf batch` (crash-safe, all-or-nothing) |\n188\t| `br show` just to get `.labels` field | `bf labels <id>` (direct `SELECT` on labels table) |\n189\t| N × `br label add` loops (N processes) | `bf label <id> add l1 l2 l3` (one transaction) |\n190\t\n191\tBackward compatibility — install `br` as a symlink:\n192\t\n193\t```bash\n194\tln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n195\t```\n196\t\n197\t---\n198\t\n199\t## Compatibility\n200\t\n201\t`bf` reads and writes the same SQLite schema and JSONL format as `br`. A workspace used by `bf` can be read by `br` and vice versa. Verified by three test suites (see plan §Compatibility Verification):\n202\t\n203\t1. **JSONL round-trip**: `br list --format json` == `bf list --format json` on the same workspace\n204\t2. **SQLite compat**: `bf` writes, `br` reads, `br doctor` passes\n205\t3. **Claim race**: 20 concurrent `bf claim` processes, 10 beads — exactly 10 claims succeed, no bead claimed twice\n206\t\n207\t---\n208\t\n209\t## Reliability\n210\t\n211\t- **rusqlite** (standard SQLite C library) — not FrankenSQLite, which had known corruption under concurrent writes\n212\t- **WAL mode** — concurrent reads never block writes; writers queue, not corrupt\n213\t- **`BEGIN IMMEDIATE`** — acquires write lock before any reads; eliminates TOCTOU\n214\t- **`SQLITE_BUSY` retry** — exponential backoff up to 5 retries; converts contention spikes to short waits\n215\t- **JSONL always authoritative** — `bf doctor --repair` rebuilds `beads.db` from `issues.jsonl` at any time\n216\t\n217\t---\n218\t\n219\t## Implementation\n220\t\n221\tSee [`docs/plan/plan.md`](plan/plan.md) for the complete implementation plan including:\n222\t\n223\t- Exact SQLite schema (all 13 tables + indexes)\n224\t- `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` retry wrapper implementation\n225\t- Critical path CTE algorithm\n226\t- Velocity stats schema and claim scoring formula\n227\t- Compatibility verification test suites\n228\t- NEEDLE integration specifics (file, line numbers, before/after)\n229\t\n230\t---\n231\t\n232\t## Build & Deploy\n233\t\n234\tBuilt via Argo Workflows on `iad-ci`. WorkflowTemplate: `bead-forge-build` in `jedarden/declarative-config`.\n235\t\n236\t```bash\n237\t# Install\n238\tcurl -L https://github.com/jedarden/bead-forge/releases/latest/download/bf-linux-x86_64 \\\n239\t  -o ~/.local/bin/bf && chmod +x ~/.local/bin/bf\n240\t\n241\t# Drop-in replace br\n242\tln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n243\t\n244\t# Verify\n245\tbf --version\n246\tbf init --prefix bf\n247\tbf create \"first bead\" --type task\n248\t```\n249\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"11951734-0c82-4f04-8dda-a530c712546f","uuid":"471dbee8-c519-4fae-93e5-c1476bf2debe","timestamp":"2026-05-01T13:32:15.729Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/docs/README.md","content":"# bead-forge (`bf`)\n\n![bead-forge hero](hero.png)\n\n**bead-forge** is a Rust CLI that replaces `br` (beads_rust) as the bead management tool for AI-supervised coding workflows. It is a drop-in replacement for `br` — every command, flag, and output format is identical — with one critical improvement: **concurrent claiming is correct**.\n\n---\n\n## The Problem It Solves\n\n`br` has a race condition at its core. Claiming a bead requires two separate operations:\n\n```\nbr ready --json          # read candidates (snapshot)\n  ↓  (race window)\nbr update <id> --status in_progress --assignee <worker>\n  ↓  (race window)\nbr show <id>             # verify the claim stuck\n```\n\nWith 20 NEEDLE workers running simultaneously, multiple workers read the same candidate list, all select the same top bead, and all issue `br update`. SQLite last-writer-wins — 19 workers hold phantom claims on beads they don't actually own.\n\n`bf` eliminates this with a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` SQLite transaction:\n\n```\nbf claim --assignee worker-7 --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --harness needle --json\n```\n\nThe entire read-score-update sequence runs inside one write lock. No two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\nSQLite (live store)                    issues.jsonl (git artifact)\n    │                                        │\n    │  all reads and writes                  │  exported by bf sync --flush\n    │                                        │  imported by bf sync --import\n    ▼                                        ▼\n  beads.db                            .beads/issues.jsonl\n  (gitignored)                        (committed to git)\n```\n\n**SQLite is the live database.** JSONL is a derived snapshot committed to git for backup, cross-machine sharing, and recovery. `beads.db` is rebuilt at any time with `bf doctor --repair`.\n\n**No daemon. No server. No flock.** Each `bf` invocation is self-contained: open DB, acquire write lock, execute, release, exit. SQLite WAL mode enables concurrent reads during writes.\n\n---\n\n## Key Features\n\n### Atomic Concurrent Claiming\n\n```\nWorker 1: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT + score → UPDATE winner → COMMIT\nWorker 2: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked until Worker 1 commits) → SELECT → ...\n...\nWorker 20: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT → NONE (queue empty) → COMMIT\n```\n\nThe entire claim pipeline — stale reclamation, candidate scoring, winner selection, status update, event recording — runs in a single SQLite write transaction. Guaranteed correct under any concurrency level.\n\n### Critical Path Scoring\n\n`bf` computes the **float** of each bead: how many hops it can slip before delaying its epic. Beads on the critical path (`float == 0`) receive a 1000-point bonus in the claim scorer, ensuring the fleet works on the most impactful beads automatically — without human priority curation.\n\n```\n$ bf critical-path bf-epic-123\n\n  float=0  [bf-a3f8] Implement auth token refresh        in_progress\n  float=0  [bf-b2c1] Fix concurrent session state bug    open\n  float=2  [bf-d5e3] Update API documentation            open\n```\n\n### Velocity-Aware Scoring\n\nWorkers declare their composition when claiming:\n\n```bash\nbf claim --assignee worker-7 \\\n         --model claude-sonnet-4-6 \\\n         --harness needle \\\n         --harness-version 0.5.2 \\\n         --json\n```\n\n`bf` tracks close times per `(model, harness, issue_type)` in `velocity_stats`. The claim scorer weights candidates by `impact / expected_duration` — the fleet maximizes throughput per unit time, not just raw priority. After 10+ completions per cohort, routing becomes measurably smarter.\n\n```\n$ bf velocity\n\nModel                  Harness   Type     Samples  p50    p90\nclaude-opus-4-7        needle    task     142      8m     22m\nclaude-sonnet-4-6      needle    task     87       18m    45m\nclaude-haiku-4-5       needle    task     23       35m    2h10m\n```\n\n### Atomic Batch Operations\n\nReplaces NEEDLE's crash-unsafe create+dep chains:\n\n```bash\n# NEEDLE mitosis: split 1 bead into N children atomically\n# Method 1: Dedicated mitosis command (recommended)\nbf mitosis bf-a3f8 \\\n  --children '[\n    {\"title\": \"Implement login handler\", \"type\": \"task\", \"priority\": 2},\n    {\"title\": \"Add session tests\", \"type\": \"task\", \"priority\": 2}\n  ]' \\\n  --reason \"Split into children\"\n\n# Method 2: Direct batch with placeholder references\n# Use @0, @1, ... to reference beads created earlier in the batch\nbf batch --json '[\n  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Implement login handler\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Add session tests\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"@0\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"@1\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n  {\"op\": \"close\", \"id\": \"bf-a3f8\", \"reason\": \"Split into children\"}\n]'\n```\n\n**Placeholder references**: `@0`, `@1`, etc. resolve to the IDs of beads created earlier in the batch. You don't need to know the child IDs in advance — `bf` substitutes them automatically.\n\nAll operations execute in one `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. A crash mid-batch leaves zero partial state — SQLite rolls back automatically.\n\n### Extensible Annotations\n\nArbitrary key-value metadata on any bead, transparent to `br`:\n\n```bash\nbf annotate bf-a3f8 needle_attempt=3 needle_session=abc123 review_status=needs_review\nbf list --annotation needle_attempt=3\n```\n\n### Operation History\n\n```bash\nbf log bf-a3f8\n\n2026-04-29 10:00  CREATED     by: human                  \"Implement auth flow\"\n2026-04-29 14:30  CLAIMED     by: worker-3 (sonnet-4-6)  open → in_progress\n2026-04-29 15:45  COMMENT     by: worker-3               \"Found edge case\"\n2026-04-29 16:00  CLOSED      by: worker-3               \"Completed\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## Commands\n\n```\nbf create        --title \"...\" --type <type> --priority <N> [--label ...]\nbf list          [--status <s>] [--type <t>] [--assignee <a>] [--format json|text|toon]\nbf show          <id>\nbf update        <id> [--status <s>] [--assignee <a>] ...]\nbf close         <id> [--reason \"...\"]\nbf claim         --assignee <id> [--model <m>] [--harness <h>] [--harness-version <v>]\n                 [--workspace <path>] [--any]\nbf critical-path <epic-id>\nbf batch         [--file ops.json] [--json '[...]']\nbf annotate      <id> key=value [--remove key]\nbf log           [<id>] [--since <date>] [--actor <a>] [--diff]\nbf rotate        [--age-days N]\nbf velocity      [--model <m>] [--harness <h>]\nbf dep           add-blocker <blocker> <blockee>\nbf dep           tree <id>\nbf label         <id> add <label> [<label> ...]\nbf sync          [--flush-only] [--import-only]\nbf doctor        [--repair] [--check] [--reclaim-stale [--ttl <duration>]]\nbf init          [--prefix <p>]\nbf stats\nbf search        <query>\n```\n\nAll `br` commands work identically. `bf` is a strict superset.\n\n---\n\n## NEEDLE Integration\n\nReplace the five non-atomic `br` chains in `bead_store/mod.rs`:\n\n| Old (racy) | New (atomic) |\n|-----------|--------------|\n| `br ready` → `br update` → `br show` (3 calls, 2 race windows) | `bf claim` (1 call, 1 transaction) |\n| `br create` + `br dep add` (orphan if crash between) | `bf batch` (crash-safe, all-or-nothing) |\n| `br show` just to get `.labels` field | `bf labels <id>` (direct `SELECT` on labels table) |\n| N × `br label add` loops (N processes) | `bf label <id> add l1 l2 l3` (one transaction) |\n\nBackward compatibility — install `br` as a symlink:\n\n```bash\nln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n```\n\n---\n\n## Compatibility\n\n`bf` reads and writes the same SQLite schema and JSONL format as `br`. A workspace used by `bf` can be read by `br` and vice versa. Verified by three test suites (see plan §Compatibility Verification):\n\n1. **JSONL round-trip**: `br list --format json` == `bf list --format json` on the same workspace\n2. **SQLite compat**: `bf` writes, `br` reads, `br doctor` passes\n3. **Claim race**: 20 concurrent `bf claim` processes, 10 beads — exactly 10 claims succeed, no bead claimed twice\n\n---\n\n## Reliability\n\n- **rusqlite** (standard SQLite C library) — not FrankenSQLite, which had known corruption under concurrent writes\n- **WAL mode** — concurrent reads never block writes; writers queue, not corrupt\n- **`BEGIN IMMEDIATE`** — acquires write lock before any reads; eliminates TOCTOU\n- **`SQLITE_BUSY` retry** — exponential backoff up to 5 retries; converts contention spikes to short waits\n- **JSONL always authoritative** — `bf doctor --repair` rebuilds `beads.db` from `issues.jsonl` at any time\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\nSee [`docs/plan/plan.md`](plan/plan.md) for the complete implementation plan including:\n\n- Exact SQLite schema (all 13 tables + indexes)\n- `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` retry wrapper implementation\n- Critical path CTE algorithm\n- Velocity stats schema and claim scoring formula\n- Compatibility verification test suites\n- NEEDLE integration specifics (file, line numbers, before/after)\n\n---\n\n## Build & Deploy\n\nBuilt via Argo Workflows on `iad-ci`. WorkflowTemplate: `bead-forge-build` in `jedarden/declarative-config`.\n\n```bash\n# Install\ncurl -L https://github.com/jedarden/bead-forge/releases/latest/download/bf-linux-x86_64 \\\n  -o ~/.local/bin/bf && chmod +x ~/.local/bin/bf\n\n# Drop-in replace br\nln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n\n# Verify\nbf --version\nbf init --prefix bf\nbf create \"first bead\" --type task\n```\n","numLines":249,"startLine":1,"totalLines":249}}}

using the birthday problem formula.\n///\n/// For a given expected number of items N and hash length L with base B:\n///   P(collision) ≈ 1 - exp(-N² / (2 * B^L))\n///\n/// Solving for L given target collision probability p:\n///   L ≈ log_B(N² / (2 * -ln(1-p)))\n///\n/// We use p = 0.05 (5% collision probability) to balance br compatibility\n/// with mathematical soundness, and clamp L to [3, 8].\npub fn optimal_hash_length(existing_count: usize) -> usize {\n    if existing_count == 0 {\n        return 3;\n    }\n\n    // Target: 5% collision probability (balance between br compatibility and safety)\n    const TARGET_COLLISION_PROB: f64 = 0.05;\n    let neg_ln_one_minus_p = -((1.0 - TARGET_COLLISION_PROB).ln());\n\n    // B = 36 (base36), solve for L\n    // L = log_36(N² / (2 * -ln(1-p)))\n    let n = existing_count as f64;\n    let numerator = n * n;\n    let denominator = 2.0 * neg_ln_one_minus_p;\n    let base = 36.0_f64;\n\n    let length = (numerator / denominator).log(base).ceil() as usize;\n\n    // Clamp to [3, 8] range\n    length.clamp(3, 8)\n}\n\npub fn base36_encode(data: &[u8]) -> String {\n    let mut result = String::new();\n    let num = BigUint::from_bytes_be(data);\n    let base = BigUint::from(36u32);\n\n    if num.is_zero() {\n        return \"0\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    let mut n = num;\n    while n > BigUint::zero() {\n desired length.\n    let hash_encoded = base36_encode(&hash);\n    let truncated = hash_encoded.chars().take(len).collect::<String>();\n\n    format!(\"{}-{}\", prefix, truncated)\n}\n\npub fn   // IDs should be lowercase alphanumeric after the prefix\n        for _ in 0..100 {\n            let id = generate_id(\"bf\", 100);\n            let hash_part = id.split('-').nth(1).unwrap();\n            assert!(hash_part.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_lowercase() || c.is_ascii_digit()),\n                    \"Hash should be lowercase alphanumeric: {}\", id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_adaptive_hash_length() {\n        // Verify hash length changes with count\n        let id_100 = generate_id(\"bf\", 100);\n        let hash_100 = id_100.split('-').nth(1).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(hash_100.len(), optimal_hash_length(100));\n\n        let id_10000 = generate_id(\"bf\", 10000);\n        let hash_10000 = id_10000.split('-').nth(1).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(hash_10000.len(), optimal_hash_length(10000));\n\n        // Higher count should produce longer or equal hash\n        assert!(hash_10000.len() >= hash_100.len());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_no_collisions_10k() {\n        // Acceptance criteria: no collisions in 10k-ID corpus\n        // Use a fixed count of 10000 for all IDs - this is how adaptive hash length\n        // is meant to be used (estimate corpus size upfront)\n        let corpus_size = 10000;\n        let mut ids = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n\n        for _ in 0..corpus_size {\n            let id = generate_id(\"bf\", corpus_size);\n            assert!(ids.insert(id.clone()), \"Collision detected in {}-ID corpus\", corpus_size);\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(ids.len(), corpus_size, \"Should
